questions.stem:
—______ you ever ______ to the Shenzhen Science Museum?
—Yes, I ______ there with my classmates last month.
questions.explanation: 第一空：问句中有ever（曾经），是现在完成时的标志词，且表示"去过某地（已返回）"用have been to；第二空：答句中有last month（上个月），是明确的过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时went。干扰项C的have gone to表示"去了某地（未回）"，与语境不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The student ______ won the first prize in the English speech contest is from Class 3.
questions.explanation: 先行词是the student（指人），在定语从句中作主语，因此用关系代词who。which用于指物；whom用于指人但在从句中作宾语；whose表示"谁的"，在从句中作定语。解题口诀：先行词指人作主语，who来引导莫犹豫。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ______ to the library by the students tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 主语These old books与动词return之间是被动关系（书被归还），因此要用被动语态；时间状语tomorrow表示将来，所以用一般将来时的被动语态will be returned。被动语态基本结构：be + 过去分词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The environment in Shenzhen is getting ______ than before. The air is ______ cleaner now.
questions.explanation: 第一空：than是比较级标志词，get better表示"变得更好"，good的比较级是better；第二空：修饰比较级cleaner要用程度副词much，表示"...得多"，不能用very或more（more本身用于构成多音节词的比较级）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may ______ if too many trucks pass over it at the same time.
questions.explanation: collapse意为"倒塌、坍塌"，符合语境"桥不够坚固，支撑不了重型卡车"。fall down侧重"摔倒、跌落"；break down通常指机器"出故障"或物质"分解"；fall behind指"落后"。根据上下文"not strong enough"和"support heavy trucks"，选择collapse最准确。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ our best to reduce pollution and protect the environment around us.
questions.explanation: try one's best是固定搭配，意为"尽某人最大努力"。其他选项：make one's best不是固定搭配；do one's best虽然也有"尽力"之意，但通常用于do one's best，而题干中是our best，try更常用；give one's best表示"给出最好的"，不符合句意。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Everyone was ______ when they heard the ______ news that our school team had won the final game.
questions.explanation: -ed形容词（excited）通常修饰人，表示"感到...的"；-ing形容词（exciting）通常修饰物，表示"令人...的"。第一空主语是Everyone（人），用excited；第二空修饰news（事物），用exciting。口诀：-ed感到，-ing令人。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 根据下文"All the students were excited to take part in it"（学生们兴奋参与）可知，学校应该是"启动/开始"了一个项目。started符合语境。finished（完成）和stopped（停止）与 excited to take part 矛盾；forgot（忘记）不符合逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A School Green Project

Last month, our school (1)______ a meaningful green project called "Save Our Earth". All the students were excited to take part in it.

At first, some students thought it would be boring to pick up rubbish. (2)______, they changed their minds soon. We were divided into different groups. My group was responsible for recycling waste paper and plastic bottles. During the activity, we (3)______ over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials.

Through this project, we not only learned the importance of environmental protection but also felt (4)______ of what we had done. We (5)______ more students can join us and make our school greener in the future.

questions.explanation: 前文说"some students thought it would be boring"（一些学生觉得无聊），后文说"they changed their minds"（他们改变了想法），前后是转折关系，用However（然而）。Therefore和So表示因果，Besides表示递进，都不符合逻辑关系。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A School Green Project

Last month, our school (1)______ a meaningful green project called "Save Our Earth". All the students were excited to take part in it.

At first, some students thought it would be boring to pick up rubbish. (2)______, they changed their minds soon. We were divided into different groups. My group was responsible for recycling waste paper and plastic bottles. During the activity, we (3)______ over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials.

Through this project, we not only learned the importance of environmental protection but also felt (4)______ of what we had done. We (5)______ more students can join us and make our school greener in the future.

questions.explanation: 根据前文"recycling waste paper and plastic bottles"（回收废纸和塑料瓶）可知，此处应该是"收集"了200多公斤可回收材料。collected（收集）符合语境。produced（生产）、created（创造）、invented（发明）都与"回收垃圾"的语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A School Green Project

Last month, our school (1)______ a meaningful green project called "Save Our Earth". All the students were excited to take part in it.

At first, some students thought it would be boring to pick up rubbish. (2)______, they changed their minds soon. We were divided into different groups. My group was responsible for recycling waste paper and plastic bottles. During the activity, we (3)______ over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials.

Through this project, we not only learned the importance of environmental protection but also felt (4)______ of what we had done. We (5)______ more students can join us and make our school greener in the future.

questions.explanation: 根据前文"we not only learned the importance..."（我们不仅学到了...）可知，这是积极的体验，felt proud of（为...感到自豪）符合语境。afraid（害怕）、tired（疲倦）、sorry（抱歉）都是消极情绪，与上下文不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A School Green Project

Last month, our school (1)______ a meaningful green project called "Save Our Earth". All the students were excited to take part in it.

At first, some students thought it would be boring to pick up rubbish. (2)______, they changed their minds soon. We were divided into different groups. My group was responsible for recycling waste paper and plastic bottles. During the activity, we (3)______ over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials.

Through this project, we not only learned the importance of environmental protection but also felt (4)______ of what we had done. We (5)______ more students can join us and make our school greener in the future.

questions.explanation: 根据句意"我们______更多学生能加入我们"，hope（希望）符合语境和逻辑。decide（决定）通常用于自己做出决定，不用于希望别人做某事；refuse（拒绝）和forget（忘记）与句意相反。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A School Green Project

Last month, our school (1)______ a meaningful green project called "Save Our Earth". All the students were excited to take part in it.

At first, some students thought it would be boring to pick up rubbish. (2)______, they changed their minds soon. We were divided into different groups. My group was responsible for recycling waste paper and plastic bottles. During the activity, we (3)______ over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials.

Through this project, we not only learned the importance of environmental protection but also felt (4)______ of what we had done. We (5)______ more students can join us and make our school greener in the future.

questions.stem: How many hours do Shenzhen teenagers spend on screens daily on average according to the passage?
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据文章第一段第一句"A recent survey shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 3.5 hours on screens every day on average"可知，平均每天花3.5小时。解题技巧：细节题要精准定位，找到原文对应句子，注意数字信息。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Teenagers and Screen Time in Shenzhen

A recent survey shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 3.5 hours on screens every day on average. This includes time spent on mobile phones, computers, and TVs. Doctors are worried about this trend because too much screen time can cause eye problems and affect sleep quality.

Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect eyesight. According to this rule, every 20 minutes of screen time, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. "This simple habit can effectively reduce eye strain," Dr. Li explains. "It gives your eye muscles a chance to relax."

However, some students say it's hard to follow this rule when they have lots of online homework. "I have to stare at the screen for hours to finish my assignments," said Wang Wei, a Grade 8 student. "Sometimes my eyes feel dry and painful after studying."

Experts suggest that students should balance study and rest. They recommend taking regular breaks and doing outdoor activities for at least one hour every day. Parents are also advised to set a good example by reducing their own screen time and spending more time with their children outdoors.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li Ming's "20-20-20" rule mentioned in Paragraph 2?
options: A. We should only use screens for 20 minutes every day.
B. Looking at distant objects can help rest our eyes.
C. We need to exercise for 20 seconds every 20 minutes.
D. Teenagers should stand 20 feet away from the screen.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第二段"look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds"和"It gives your eye muscles a chance to relax"可推断，看远处物体有助于放松眼部肌肉，即帮助眼睛休息。A、C、D都是对原文的误解或过度推断。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能主观臆断。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Teenagers and Screen Time in Shenzhen

A recent survey shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 3.5 hours on screens every day on average. This includes time spent on mobile phones, computers, and TVs. Doctors are worried about this trend because too much screen time can cause eye problems and affect sleep quality.

Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect eyesight. According to this rule, every 20 minutes of screen time, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. "This simple habit can effectively reduce eye strain," Dr. Li explains. "It gives your eye muscles a chance to relax."

However, some students say it's hard to follow this rule when they have lots of online homework. "I have to stare at the screen for hours to finish my assignments," said Wang Wei, a Grade 8 student. "Sometimes my eyes feel dry and painful after studying."

Experts suggest that students should balance study and rest. They recommend taking regular breaks and doing outdoor activities for at least one hour every day. Parents are also advised to set a good example by reducing their own screen time and spending more time with their children outdoors.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "strain" in Paragraph 2 probably mean in Chinese?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文"protect eyesight"（保护视力）和"gives your eye muscles a chance to relax"（让眼部肌肉放松）可知，strain应该是指眼睛的压力或疲劳状态。解题技巧：通过上下文语境、同义词/反义词、定义解释等线索猜测词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Teenagers and Screen Time in Shenzhen

A recent survey shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 3.5 hours on screens every day on average. This includes time spent on mobile phones, computers, and TVs. Doctors are worried about this trend because too much screen time can cause eye problems and affect sleep quality.

Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect eyesight. According to this rule, every 20 minutes of screen time, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. "This simple habit can effectively reduce eye strain," Dr. Li explains. "It gives your eye muscles a chance to relax."

However, some students say it's hard to follow this rule when they have lots of online homework. "I have to stare at the screen for hours to finish my assignments," said Wang Wei, a Grade 8 student. "Sometimes my eyes feel dry and painful after studying."

Experts suggest that students should balance study and rest. They recommend taking regular breaks and doing outdoor activities for at least one hour every day. Parents are also advised to set a good example by reducing their own screen time and spending more time with their children outdoors.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Shenzhen Hospitals
B. How to Do Online Homework Quickly
C. Teenagers and Screen Time in Shenzhen
D. The Importance of Outdoor Activities
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章第一段点明主题"Shenzhen teenagers spend about 3.5 hours on screens"，全文围绕深圳青少年的屏幕使用时间、带来的问题及解决方案展开。A、B、D都只是文章细节，不能概括全文。解题技巧：最佳标题应涵盖文章核心内容，具有概括性。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Teenagers and Screen Time in Shenzhen

A recent survey shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 3.5 hours on screens every day on average. This includes time spent on mobile phones, computers, and TVs. Doctors are worried about this trend because too much screen time can cause eye problems and affect sleep quality.

Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect eyesight. According to this rule, every 20 minutes of screen time, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. "This simple habit can effectively reduce eye strain," Dr. Li explains. "It gives your eye muscles a chance to relax."

However, some students say it's hard to follow this rule when they have lots of online homework. "I have to stare at the screen for hours to finish my assignments," said Wang Wei, a Grade 8 student. "Sometimes my eyes feel dry and painful after studying."

Experts suggest that students should balance study and rest. They recommend taking regular breaks and doing outdoor activities for at least one hour every day. Parents are also advised to set a good example by reducing their own screen time and spending more time with their children outdoors.

options: A. However, many students don't know how to take proper breaks.
B. In addition, taking regular breaks can improve your memory and creativity.
C. As a result, they often feel tired and can't focus on their studies.
D. Then, divide your study time into smaller parts with short breaks.
E. On the other hand, you should also make time for physical exercise.
questions.explanation: 前文说"许多学生想取得好成绩，所以花大部分时间学习"，后文说"他们连续几小时坐在书桌前不休息"，此处用However转折，指出"然而，许多学生不知道如何正确休息"，引出下文的具体表现。As a result表示结果，通常用于描述后果而非引出新的问题。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Rest

Many students want to get good grades, so they spend most of their time studying. (17)______ They sit at their desks for hours without breaks. This is actually not good for their health or study efficiency.

(18)______ When you study for too long, your brain gets tired and you can't concentrate well. That's why you may find it harder to remember things after studying for three hours straight.

So what should we do? First, make a reasonable study plan. Don't try to finish all your work in one sitting. (19)______ For example, you can study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes. During the break, stand up, stretch your body, or look out of the window.

(20)______ Don't forget to do some sports every day. A healthy body helps you study better.

options: A. However, many students don't know how to take proper breaks.
B. In addition, taking regular breaks can improve your memory and creativity.
C. As a result, they often feel tired and can't focus on their studies.
D. Then, divide your study time into smaller parts with short breaks.
E. On the other hand, you should also make time for physical exercise.
questions.explanation: 前文说"连续几小时不休息"，后文说"当你学习太久，大脑会疲劳，无法集中注意力"，此处需要表示结果/后果的过渡句"因此，他们经常感到疲倦，无法专注于学习"。As a result表示"结果、因此"，承上启下，连接因果关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Rest

Many students want to get good grades, so they spend most of their time studying. (17)______ They sit at their desks for hours without breaks. This is actually not good for their health or study efficiency.

(18)______ When you study for too long, your brain gets tired and you can't concentrate well. That's why you may find it harder to remember things after studying for three hours straight.

So what should we do? First, make a reasonable study plan. Don't try to finish all your work in one sitting. (19)______ For example, you can study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes. During the break, stand up, stretch your body, or look out of the window.

(20)______ Don't forget to do some sports every day. A healthy body helps you study better.

options: A. However, many students don't know how to take proper breaks.
B. In addition, taking regular breaks can improve your memory and creativity.
C. As a result, they often feel tired and can't focus on their studies.
D. Then, divide your study time into smaller parts with short breaks.
E. On the other hand, you should also make time for physical exercise.
questions.explanation: 前文说"不要试图一次性完成所有作业"，后文举例"比如学习45分钟然后休息10分钟"，此处需要具体的建议/方法"然后，把你的学习时间分成几个小段，中间穿插短暂休息"。Then表示顺序，承接上文的First，引出具体做法。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Rest

Many students want to get good grades, so they spend most of their time studying. (17)______ They sit at their desks for hours without breaks. This is actually not good for their health or study efficiency.

(18)______ When you study for too long, your brain gets tired and you can't concentrate well. That's why you may find it harder to remember things after studying for three hours straight.

So what should we do? First, make a reasonable study plan. Don't try to finish all your work in one sitting. (19)______ For example, you can study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes. During the break, stand up, stretch your body, or look out of the window.

(20)______ Don't forget to do some sports every day. A healthy body helps you study better.

options: A. However, many students don't know how to take proper breaks.
B. In addition, taking regular breaks can improve your memory and creativity.
C. As a result, they often feel tired and can't focus on their studies.
D. Then, divide your study time into smaller parts with short breaks.
E. On the other hand, you should also make time for physical exercise.
questions.explanation: 后文说"别忘了每天做些运动"，此处应该引出运动/体育锻炼的话题"另一方面，你也应该腾出时间进行体育锻炼"。On the other hand用于引出另一个相关但不同方面的建议，与上文的休息建议形成并列补充关系。In addition虽然也表示补充，但后文已经明确提到sports，用On the other hand更强调这是另一个重要方面。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Rest

Many students want to get good grades, so they spend most of their time studying. (17)______ They sit at their desks for hours without breaks. This is actually not good for their health or study efficiency.

(18)______ When you study for too long, your brain gets tired and you can't concentrate well. That's why you may find it harder to remember things after studying for three hours straight.

So what should we do? First, make a reasonable study plan. Don't try to finish all your work in one sitting. (19)______ For example, you can study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes. During the break, stand up, stretch your body, or look out of the window.

(20)______ Don't forget to do some sports every day. A healthy body helps you study better.
